core_behavior.note: All three models agree on sorting ascending, deduplication, and empty list behavior.

inputs.note: Model A explicitly rejects non-integers and mixed types; Model B says behavior is undefined for non-integers; Model C says non-integer handling is not required; none address null input explicitly in this field.

outputs.note: All three models agree on a sorted, deduplicated list returned in ascending order with empty list returning empty list.

assumptions.note: Model A explicitly rejects floats and addresses null; Model B assumes a stable sort; Model C assumes non-integer handling is not required; models diverge on unstated design choices around type handling and algorithm properties.

failure_modes.note: Model A raises a null/argument error for null input and a type error for non-list types; Model B raises ValueError for non-integers and adds MemoryError and RuntimeError; Model C raises a generic unexpected error path; error types and coverage differ across models.

edge_cases.note: All models cover empty, single-element, and all-duplicates cases but none explicitly address null input or non-integer elements as edge cases.

security_constraints.note: Model A has no security constraints; Models B and C both specify input validation and information exposure concerns.

performance_constraints.note: Model A has no performance constraints; Model B specifies O(n log n) relative to unique element count and a 10,000-element threshold under 1 second; Model C specifies O(n log n) relative to total input size with no numeric threshold.

summary: ASM (Assumption Divergence) appeared most frequently, tied with one occurrence each of OMI, CON-S, and ERR across the eight fields.
